samples.gvd:
module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);

reg state;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (~rst_n) begin
        state <= 0;
        wave <= 0;
    end
    else begin
        case (state)
            0: begin
                if (wave == 31) begin
                    state <= 1;
                end
                else begin
                    wave <= wave + 1;
                end
            end
            1: begin
                if (wave == 0) begin
                    state <= 0;
                end
                else begin
                    wave <= wave - 1;
                end
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
```xml
<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave Signal Generator</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="STATE_IDLE" value="0">Initial state where waveform increments.</state>
        <state name="STATE_DEC" value="1">State where waveform decrements.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="state" width="1" initial_value="0">State register controlling waveform generation.</register>
        <register id="wave" width="5" initial_value="0">Waveform register representing the generated waveform.</register>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        Always block triggered on posedge clk or negedge rst_n.
        On reset, clear state and wave registers.
        State machine controls waveform generation:
        - In STATE_IDLE, increment wave until it reaches 31, then transition to STATE_DEC.
        - In STATE_DEC, decrement wave until it reaches 0, then return to STATE_IDLE.
    </implementation>
</module>
```
